insurance: War risk premiums **4-10% of vessel value per voyage** (vs 0.2-0.5% pre-crisis; 20-50x increase); International Group of P&I Clubs suspended standard cover effective Mar 5; Lloyd's Joint War Committee redesignated entire Arabian Gulf as conflict zone; estimated $8-$14/bbl "geopolitical risk premium" added to global oil prices; for $120M VLCC, ~$6M-12M premium per transit

thingsToWatch.text: JPM 25天储存缓冲已耗尽（第30天），海湾产油国被迫减产
Brent突破$100（当前$115.27），通胀压力加大

**航运量回升 + 中国船只通行 + 外交僵局持续** — Trump 10-day delay on threatened strike against Iran energy infrastructure to April 6, 2026 remains in effect; **Pakistan four-party meeting (Turkey, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Pakistan) continues March 29-30** focused on ceasefire proposals; Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar states both US and Iran have expressed confidence in Pakistan's mediation role; Iran agreed to permit 20 additional Pakistani-flagged vessels as confidence-building measure (2 ships/day); US 15-point ceasefire proposal conveyed through Pakistan mediator (sanctions relief, nuclear rollback, missile limits, Hormuz access); Iran FM Araghchi explicitly denies negotiations with US, characterizes US messages as "not a conversation nor a negotiation"; Iran parliament drafting bill to formalize transit fees and codify sovereignty ("Tehran Toll Booth" legal framework); Iran reportedly submitted 5-point counter-proposal demanding war reparations and recognition of sovereignty over Strait; Iran parliament speaker Qalibaf dismisses talks as "cover" and warns against US ground invasion; Malaysia announces 7 stranded vessels will depart; Indonesia and Thailand report positive talks for safe passage; **March 30: Two Chinese ultra-large container carriers (CSCL Indian Ocean, CSCL Arctic Ocean) successfully transited** after March 27 attempt was aborted when IRGC turned back three container ships; Chinese-owned bulk carrier Mac Hope also transited; **JMIC reports 11 transits/day March 28-29, up from 1-2 previously**; GPS/GNSS/AIS interference significantly reduced, now "sporadic and localised"

situationSummary: 海峡通行量11艘/日，Brent $115.27，封锁第30天。**航运量回升 + 中国船只通行 + 外交僵局持续** — Trump 10-day delay on threatened strike against Iran energy infrastructure to April 6, 2026 remains in effect; **Pakistan four-party meeting (Turkey, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Pakistan) continues March 29-30** focused on ceasefire proposals; Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar states both US and Iran have expressed confidence in Pakistan's mediation role; Iran agreed to permit 20 additional Pakistani-flagged vessels as confidence-building measure (2 ships/day); US 15-point ceasefire proposal conveyed through Pakistan mediator (sanctions relief, nuclear rollback, missile limits, Hormuz access); Iran FM Araghchi explicitly denies negotiations with US, characterizes US messages as "not a conversation nor a negotiation"; Iran parliament drafting bill to formalize transit fees and codify sovereignty ("Tehran Toll Booth" legal framework); Iran reportedly submitted 5-point counter-proposal demanding war reparations and recognition of sovereignty over Strait; Iran parliament speaker Qalibaf dismisses talks as "cover" and warns against US ground invasion; Malaysia announces 7 stranded vessels will depart; Indonesia and Thailand report positive talks for safe passage; **March 30: Two Chinese ultra-large container carriers (CSCL Indian Ocean, CSCL Arctic Ocean) successfully transited** after March 27 attempt was aborted when IRGC turned back three container ships; Chinese-owned bulk carrier Mac Hope also transited; **JMIC reports 11 transits/day March 28-29, up from 1-2 previously**; GPS/GNSS/AIS interference significantly reduced, now "sporadic and localised" **Selective blockade maintained + "Tehran Toll Booth" operational + Denies negotiations + Legislative codification advancing + Pakistan exemption granted + Chinese vessels approved** — IRGC: "Strait under Iran's control, freedom of navigation subject to Iran-defined frameworks"; vetting system operational for approved vessels (China, Russia, Iraq, Pakistan, Turkey, Malaysia, Thailand, India); FM Araghchi: "Strait open, but closed to our enemies"; Iran denies negotiations with US despite Pakistan confirmation; ~400-2,000 vessels waiting outside strait; monetizing transit fees (~$2M-2.8M/vessel, some in yuan); Iran maintains own oil exports via "dark" transits without AIS (16M+ barrels to China since early March); Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf: "Situation in Strait will not revert to pre-war status"; IRGC warns complete closure if US attacks Iranian power plants; **Parliament advancing legislation** to formalize fees and codify sovereignty; IRGC expands influence over Iran decision-making amidst leadership changes; senior Iranian official: "path forward possible if realism prevails in Washington"; Iran submitted 5-point counter-proposal (war reparations, sovereignty recognition); **March 29: Pakistan announces Iran allows 20 additional Pakistani ships (2/day)**; **March 30: Chinese container ships transit successfully**; IRGC announced Mar 27 it prevented three container ships from passing through

timeline.text: **航运量回升 + 中国船只通行 + 外交僵局持续** — Trump 10-day delay on threatened strike against Iran energy infrastructure to April 6, 2026 remains in effect; **Pakistan four-party meeting (Turkey, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Pakistan) continues March 29-30** focused on ceasefire proposals; Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar states both US and Iran have expressed confidence in Pakistan's mediation role; Iran agreed to permit 20 additional Pakistani-flagged vessels as confidence-building measure (2 ships/day); US 15-point ceasefire proposal conveyed through Pakistan mediator (sanctions relief, nuclear rollback, missile limits, Hormuz access); Iran FM Araghchi explicitly denies negotiations with US, characterizes US messages as "not a conversation nor a negotiation"; Iran parliament drafting bill to formalize transit fees and codify sovereignty ("Tehran Toll Booth" legal framework); Iran reportedly submitted 5-point counter-proposal demanding war reparations and recognition of sovereignty over Strait; Iran parliament speaker Qalibaf dismisses talks as "cover" and warns against US ground invasion; Malaysia announces 7 stranded vessels will depart; Indonesia and Thailand report positive talks for safe passage; **March 30: Two Chinese ultra-large container carriers (CSCL Indian Ocean, CSCL Arctic Ocean) successfully transited** after March 27 attempt was aborted when IRGC turned back three container ships; Chinese-owned bulk carrier Mac Hope also transited; **JMIC reports 11 transits/day March 28-29, up from 1-2 previously**; GPS/GNSS/AIS interference significantly reduced, now "sporadic and localised"

**Selective blockade maintained + "Tehran Toll Booth" operational + Denies negotiations + Legislative codification advancing + Pakistan exemption granted + Chinese vessels approved** — IRGC: "Strait under Iran's control, freedom of navigation subject to Iran-defined frameworks"; vetting system operational for approved vessels (China, Russia, Iraq, Pakistan, Turkey, Malaysia, Thailand, India); FM Araghchi: "Strait open, but closed to our enemies"; Iran denies negotiations with US despite Pakistan confirmation; ~400-2,000 vessels waiting outside strait; monetizing transit fees (~$2M-2.8M/vessel, some in yuan); Iran maintains own oil exports via "dark" transits without AIS (16M+ barrels to China since early March); Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf: "Situation in Strait will not revert to pre-war status"; IRGC warns complete closure if US attacks Iranian power plants; **Parliament advancing legislation** to formalize fees and codify sovereignty; IRGC expands influence over Iran decision-making amidst leadership changes; senior Iranian official: "path forward possible if realism prevails in Washington"; Iran submitted 5-point counter-proposal (war reparations, sovereignty recognition); **March 29: Pakistan announces Iran allows 20 additional Pakistani ships (2/day)**; **March 30: Chinese container ships transit successfully**; IRGC announced Mar 27 it prevented three container ships from passing through

**Coalition fragmented + US military operations ongoing + Trump extension** — 22 nations condemn Iran's attacks and de facto closure; CENTCOM mine-laying prevention (16+ Iranian mine-laying ships destroyed); JMIC threat level: CRITICAL; GPS jamming widespread but reduced March 27-28; 22+ confirmed maritime incidents since Mar 1; **Trump extension to April 6**; US officials report dozen Iranian naval mines deployed; Israel strikes Tehran naval facilities; US employs A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters to target Iranian fast-attack boats and drones; 10,000+ targets struck, 92% of Iran's largest military assets destroyed; UAE announced March 27 willingness to join multinational naval force to reopen Hormuz; UK considering deploying mine-clearing vessel with advanced drones; US CENTCOM announced March 29 arrival of amphibious assault ship with thousands of Marines to Middle East
